title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data breaches, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Conduct an immediate review and hardening of CI/CD pipelines, including implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) and role-based access controls.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor and detect malicious activities in cloud environments.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in secure, immutable storage to mitigate the impact of ransomware attacks.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk as described. However, potential risks could arise if malicious actors modify or distribute a compromised version of the tool, or if organizations overly rely on it without proper validation of its findings.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and ensure it is obtained from a trusted repository before use.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other security practices, not as a sole dependency scanner.
Monitor for any updates or advisories regarding the tool to ensure continued safe usage.

title: Spring MVC and WebFlux applications are vulnerable to stream corruption when using Server-Sent...
description: Spring MVC and WebFlux applications are vulnerable to stream corruption when using Server-Sent Events (SSE). This issue affects Spring Foundation: from 7.0.0 through 7.0.5, from 6.2.0 through 6.2.16, from 6.1.0 through 6.1.25, from 5.3.0 through 5.3.46.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22735.

title: Use of Java scripting engine enabled (e.g. JRuby, Jython) template views in Spring MVC and Spring...
description: Use of Java scripting engine enabled (e.g. JRuby, Jython) template views in Spring MVC and Spring WebFlux applications can result in disclosure of content from files outside the configured locations for script template views. This issue affects Spring Framework: from 7.0.0 through 7.0.5, from 6.2.0 through 6.2.16, from 6.1.0 through 6.1.25, from 5.3.0 through 5.3.46.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22737.

title: Spring Boot applications with Actuator can be vulnerable to an "Authentication Bypass"...
description: Spring Boot applications with Actuator can be vulnerable to an "Authentication Bypass" vulnerability when an application endpoint that requires authentication is declared under the path used by the CloudFoundry Actuator endpoints. This issue affects Spring Security: from 4.0.0 through 4.0.3, from 3.5.0 through 3.5.11, from 3.4.0 through 3.4.14, from 3.3.0 through 3.3.17, from 2.7.0 through 2.7.31.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22733.

title: Spring Boot applications with Actuator can be vulnerable to an "Authentication Bypass"...
description:
Spring Boot applications with Actuator can be vulnerable to an "Authentication Bypass" vulnerability when an application endpoint that requires authentication is declared under a specific path, already configured for a Health Group additional path.
This issue affects Spring Boot: from 4.0 before 4.0.3, from 3.5 before 3.5.11, from 3.4 before 3.4.15.
This CVE is similar but not equivalent to CVE-2026-22733, as the conditions for exploit and vulnerable versions are different.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22731.

title: 1-byte OOB heap read in wc_PKCS7_DecodeEnvelopedData via zero-length encrypted content. A...
description: 1-byte OOB heap read in wc_PKCS7_DecodeEnvelopedData via zero-length encrypted content. A vulnerability existed in wolfSSL 5.8.4 and earlier, where a 1-byte out-of-bounds heap read in wc_PKCS7_DecodeEnvelopedData could be triggered by a crafted CMS EnvelopedData message with zero-length encrypted content. Note that PKCS7 support is disabled by default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a 1-byte out-of-bounds heap read, which could potentially lead to information disclosure or application crashes. However, the impact is limited as PKCS7 support is disabled by default in wolfSSL, and exploitation requires a crafted CMS EnvelopedData message.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of wolfSSL (5.8.5 or later) where the vulnerability is patched.
Verify that PKCS7 support is disabled if not explicitly required in your application.
Conduct a security review of any applications or systems using wolfSSL to ensure no exposure to this vulnerability.

title: Xerte Online Toolkits versions 3.14 and earlier contain an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload...
description: Xerte Online Toolkits versions 3.14 and earlier contain an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload vulnerability in the template import functionality. The issue exists in /website_code/php/import/import.php where missing authentication checks allow an attacker to upload a crafted ZIP archive disguised as a project template. The archive can contain a malicious PHP payload placed in the media/ directory, which is extracted into a web-accessible USER-FILES/{projectID}--{targetFolder}/ path. An attack

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32985.

title: A security issue was discovered in ingress-nginx where a combination of Ingress annotations can...
description: A security issue was discovered in ingress-nginx where a combination of Ingress annotations can be used to inject configuration into nginx. This can lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the ingress-nginx controller, and disclosure of Secrets accessible to the controller. (Note that in the default installation, the controller can access all Secrets cluster-wide.)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ingress-nginx allows attackers to inject malicious configurations into nginx, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution and unauthorized access to sensitive Secrets across the cluster. This poses a significant risk to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ingress-nginx to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-4342.
Audit and review Ingress annotations in existing configurations to ensure no malicious or unintended configurations are present.
Restrict the scope of Secrets accessible to the ingress-nginx controller by implementing least privilege principles.

title: When applications specify HTTP response headers for servlet applications using Spring Security,...
description:
When applications specify HTTP response headers for servlet applications using Spring Security, there is the possibility that the HTTP Headers will not be written. 
This issue affects Spring Security: from 5.7.0 through 5.7.21, from 5.8.0 through 5.8.23, from 6.3.0 through 6.3.14, from 6.4.0 through 6.4.14, from 6.5.0 through 6.5.8, from 7.0.0 through 7.0.3.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Spring Security can result in HTTP response headers not being written as intended. This could lead to security headers such as Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, or others being omitted, exposing applications to attacks like clickjacking, XSS, and other vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Spring Security to a patched version outside the affected ranges.
Conduct a thorough review of HTTP response headers in your application to ensure critical security headers are being applied correctly.
Implement additional runtime monitoring to detect and log missing or malformed HTTP headers in production environments.

title: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command ('command injection') in Microsoft...
description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command ('command injection') in Microsoft Bing Images allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely via command injection, potentially leading to data breaches, system compromise, or further exploitation of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by Microsoft to address CVE-2026-32194.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using Microsoft Bing Images to identify potential exploitation and monitor for unusual activity.
Implement network-level protections, such as web application firewalls (WAFs), to detect and block malicious command injection attempts.

title: Parse Server has an auth provider validation bypass on login via partial authData
description:
### Impact

An authentication bypass vulnerability allows an attacker to log in as any user who has linked a third-party authentication provider, without knowing the user's credentials. The attacker only needs to know the user's provider ID to gain full access to their account, including a valid session token.

This affects Parse Server deployments where the server option `allowExpiredAuthDataToken` is set to `true`. The default value is `false`.

### Patches

Auth providers are now always valid

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33409.

title: Scriban Affected by Memory Exhaustion (OOM) via Unbounded String Generation (Denial of Service)
description:
`TemplateContext.LimitToString` defaults to `0` (unlimited). While Scriban implements a default `LoopLimit` of 1000, an attacker can still cause massive memory allocation via exponential string growth. Doubling a string for just 30 iterations generates over 1GB of text, instantly exhausting heap memory and crashing the host process. Because no output size limit is enforced, repeated string concatenation results in exponential memory growth.

**Proof of Concept (PoC):**
The following payload exec

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: Scriban has an Infinite Recursion during Object Rendering Leads to Stack Overflow and Process Crash (Denial of Service)
description:
When Scriban renders an object that contains a circular reference, it traverses the object's members infinitely. Because the `ObjectRecursionLimit` property defaults to unlimited, this behavior exhausts the thread's stack space, triggering an uncatchable `StackOverflowException` that immediately terminates the hosting process.

When rendering objects (e.g., `{{ obj }}`), the Scriban rendering engine recursively inspects and formats the object's properties. To prevent infinite loops caused by dee

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Scriban has Uncontrolled Recursion in Parser Leads to Stack Overflow and Process Crash (Denial of Service)
description:
Scriban is vulnerable to an uncontrolled process crash resulting in a Denial of Service. Because the recursive-descent parser does not enforce a default limit on expression depth, an attacker who controls template input can craft a heavily nested template that triggers a `StackOverflowException`. In .NET, a `StackOverflowException` cannot be caught by standard `try-catch` blocks, resulting in the immediate and ungraceful termination of the entire hosting process. 

Scriban utilizes a recursive-d

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service by crashing the hosting process through a crafted template input. This can disrupt services relying on Scriban for template processing, leading to downtime and potential reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Implement input validation to restrict template depth and complexity before processing.
Upgrade Scriban to a patched version if available, or apply a custom patch to enforce a recursion depth limit in the parser.
Monitor and log template input for suspicious activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: Out-of-bounds read in ALPN parsing due to incomplete validation. wolfSSL 5.8.4 and earlier...
description: Out-of-bounds read in ALPN parsing due to incomplete validation. wolfSSL 5.8.4 and earlier contained an out-of-bounds read in ALPN handling when built with ALPN enabled (HAVE_ALPN / --enable-alpn). A crafted ALPN protocol list could trigger an out-of-bounds read, leading to a potential process crash (denial of service). Note that ALPN is disabled by default, but is enabled for these 3rd party compatibility features: enable-apachehttpd, enable-bind, enable-curl, enable-haproxy, enable-hitch, enab

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in wolfSSL versions 5.8.4 and earlier allows an attacker to exploit an out-of-bounds read in ALPN parsing, potentially causing a denial of service (process crash). This could disrupt services relying on wolfSSL for secure communication, particularly if ALPN is enabled for third-party compatibility features.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to wolfSSL version 5.8.5 or later, where this vulnerability has been patched.
Disable ALPN if it is not explicitly required for your use case to reduce exposure to this vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of third-party integrations (e.g., Apache HTTPD, cURL, HAProxy) to ensure they are updated and configured securely.

title: Heap Overflow in TLS 1.3 ECH parsing. An integer underflow existed in ECH extension parsing logic...
description: Heap Overflow in TLS 1.3 ECH parsing. An integer underflow existed in ECH extension parsing logic when calculating a buffer length, which resulted in writing beyond the bounds of an allocated buffer. Note that in wolfSSL, ECH is off by default, and the ECH standard is still evolving.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3549.

title: An integer overflow vulnerability existed in the static function wolfssl_add_to_chain, that...
description: An integer overflow vulnerability existed in the static function wolfssl_add_to_chain, that caused heap corruption when certificate data was written out of bounds of an insufficiently sized certificate buffer. wolfssl_add_to_chain is called by these API: wolfSSL_CTX_add_extra_chain_cert, wolfSSL_CTX_add1_chain_cert, wolfSSL_add0_chain_cert. These API are enabled for 3rd party compatibility features: enable-opensslall, enable-opensslextra, enable-lighty, enable-stunnel, enable-nginx, enable-hapro

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3229.

title: Improper neutralization of special elements used in an os command ('os command injection') in...
description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in an os command ('os command injection') in Microsoft Bing Images allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32191.

title: Missing required cryptographic step in the TLS 1.3 client HelloRetryRequest handshake logic in...
description: Missing required cryptographic step in the TLS 1.3 client HelloRetryRequest handshake logic in wolfSSL could lead to a compromise in the confidentiality of TLS-protected communications via a crafted HelloRetryRequest followed by a ServerHello message that omits the required key_share extension, resulting in derivation of predictable traffic secrets from (EC)DHE shared secret. This issue does not affect the client's authentication of the server during TLS handshakes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the TLS 1.3 handshake logic in wolfSSL could allow an attacker to compromise the confidentiality of TLS-protected communications by deriving predictable traffic secrets. However, the issue does not affect the client's ability to authenticate the server, limiting the overall impact.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of wolfSSL that addresses CVE-2026-3230.
Conduct a security review of systems and applications using wolfSSL to ensure proper cryptographic configurations.
Monitor for unusual or suspicious traffic patterns that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: Heap-based buffer overflow in the KCAPI ECC code path of wc_ecc_import_x963_ex() in wolfSSL...
description: Heap-based buffer overflow in the KCAPI ECC code path of wc_ecc_import_x963_ex() in wolfSSL wolfcrypt allows a remote attacker to write attacker-controlled data past the bounds of the pubkey_raw buffer via a crafted oversized EC public key point. The WOLFSSL_KCAPI_ECC code path copies the input to key->pubkey_raw (132 bytes) using XMEMCPY without a bounds check, unlike the ATECC code path which includes a length validation. This can be triggered during TLS key exchange when a malicious peer send

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a remote attacker to exploit a heap-based buffer overflow in the wolfSSL library, potentially leading to memory corruption, application crashes, or remote code execution. However, exploitation requires a crafted oversized EC public key point during a TLS key exchange, which limits the attack surface.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of wolfSSL that includes a patch for CVE-2026-4395.
Implement input validation to ensure that EC public key points are properly checked before processing.
Monitor TLS connections for anomalous or oversized EC public key points and log suspicious activity.

description: IBM Sterling Partner Engagement Manager 6.2.3.0 through 6.2.3.5 and 6.2.4.0 through 6.2.4.2 could allow an attacker to obtain sensitive information from the query string of an HTTP GET method to process a request which could be obtained using man in the middle techniques.

title: CVE-2025-14504 - IBM Sterling B2B Integrator and IBM Sterling File Gateway 6.1.0.0 through 6.1.2.7_2, 6.2.0.0 through
description: IBM Sterling B2B Integrator and IBM Sterling File Gateway 6.1.0.0 through 6.1.2.7_2, 6.2.0.0 through 6.2.0.5_1, 6.2.1.0 through 6.2.1.1_1, and 6.2.2.0 is vulnerable to cross-site scripting. This vulnerability allows an authenticated user to embed arbitrary JavaScript code in the Web UI thus altering the intended functionality potentially leading to credentials disclosure within a trusted session.

title: CVE-2025-12455 - Observable response discrepancy vulnerability in OpenText™ Vertica allows Password Brute Forcing.

Observable response discrepancy vulnerability in OpenText™ Vertica allows Password Brute Forcing.  
The vulnerability could lead to Password Brute Forcing in Vertica management console application.This issue affects Vertica: from 10.0 through 10.X, from 11.0 through 11.X, from 12.0 through 12.X.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-12455.

The vulnerability could lead to Reflected XSS attack of cross-site scripting in Vertica management console application.This issue affects Vertica: from 10.0 through 10.X, from 11.0 through 11.X, from 12.0 through 12.X, from 23.0 through 23.X, from 24.0 through 24.X, from 25.1.0 through 25.1.X.
